item.title: Real Madrid’s rolling crisis
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: why the market looks ‘soft’ for the Seattle Seahawks

item.title: What life is like on the stranded ships of the Gulf
item.description: As leaders debate how to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the voices of seafarers have been largely absent. Horatio Clare reports on the hidden world behind a global crisis

item.title: Jamie Dimon and his peers should beware of ending up on the UK ballot
item.description: There is a fine line between being ‘Concerned of Canary Wharf’ and becoming the subject for political discord yourself

item.title: Tech titans should pick up the phone — and so should the rest of us
item.description: The lesson from Elon Musk’s case against OpenAI is that writing things down can be embarrassing

item.title: AI boom could end the de-equitisation ‘put’
item.description: This US bull market has not been accompanied by the usual deluge of equity issuance. Until now

item.title: Trump’s Hormuz ship insurance facility has done $0 business
item.description: US president’s $40bn scheme has had zero uptake due to absence of naval escorts through key waterway

item.title: Nato to press Europe’s arms makers to boost investment and production
item.description: Alliance chief Mark Rutte set to meet defence groups at meeting in Brussels next week

item.title: Why doesn’t my credit score add up?
item.description: The bizarre system of credit scoring can punish the prudent and indebted alike

item.title: The messy, chaotic and possibly quixotic quest to phase out fossil fuels
item.description: The inside story of a climate summit unlike any other

item.title: The end of the mortgage broker?
item.description: Home loans and the buying process are ripe for disruption by technology

item.title: Why the IPO of bike-sharing app Lime is no lemon
item.description: At a mooted $2bn enterprise value, Uber-backed company would trade at 28 times last year’s operating profit

item.title: Can you make money from Star Wars toys?
item.description: Next Friday’s release of new film ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’ has collectors on high alert

item.title: Do you have a mango dealer?
item.description: You have to be in the know when sourcing the ‘king’ of these fruits

item.title: Deal rush in rare earths as west seeks to loosen China’s grip
item.description: Small group of companies compete for assets as they seek first-mover advantage in ‘very hot’ market

item.title: NextEra and Dominion in talks over tie-up to create $400bn US utility giant
item.description: Discussions come amid booming demand for electricity to power data centres

item.title: US federal prosecutors scrutinise BlackRock private credit fund
item.description: Wall Street’s top cops examine valuation practices at BlackRock TCP Capital Corp

item.title: Boeing shares slide as Trump’s China summit deals disappoint
item.description: US president says Beijing will expand agriculture and oil purchases

item.title: ‘Botox’ boom shows no sign of sagging even as inflation hits luxury sector
item.description: Dermatology giant Galderma says customers are prioritising aesthetic jabs despite the squeeze on disposable incomes

item.title: Carney strikes deal over new Canadian pipeline
item.description: Carbon tax deal lays the land for increased oil and gas production and shipping of fossil fuels to Asia

item.title: Crypto CFDs are here save the City, apparently
item.description: Panmure Liberum goes back to the future

item.title: Stockpickers: Victrex, Marston’s, Vodafone
item.description: Our experts discuss which companies to buy, sell or hold this week

item.title: Directors’ Deals: Softcat finance chief buys in ahead of AI boost
item.description: Profit rises amid increased demand for cyber security products

item.title: Peru run-off pits autocrat’s daughter against leftwinger
item.description: Polarised race between rightwing Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sánchez to take place on June 7

item.title: Ovo founder in line for £30mn payout ahead of Eon sale
item.description: Stephen Fitzpatrick to receive payment for brand royalty fees under arrangement with retail energy company he started

item.title: QVC’s creditors have a nasty case of buyer’s remorse
item.description: Once a household name, the home-shopping TV network has filed for bankruptcy protection

item.title: EY retracts study after researchers discover AI hallucinations
item.description: Incident is latest example of professional services firm being led astray by new technology

item.title: Small financial group close to Trump family plots $200mn Venezuela Spac deal
item.description: Yorkville seeks investment to capitalise on improving ‘commercial conditions’ in Latin America

item.title: Are we thinking about AI and productivity all wrong?
item.description: Self-reported estimates about how quickly work can be completed are not the most meaningful metric

item.title: Starbucks to take $400mn charge as it cuts more corporate staff
item.description: Sales have rebounded for coffee chain but renovations and barista hires under turnaround plan have come at a cost

item.title: Britain’s padel boom attracts converts — and cowboys
item.description: Planning restrictions have served as a brake on faster growth for the sport
